---
title: "Sam Hillman"
description: "Real-world evidence and AI scientist in oncology drug development — observational study design, causal inference, and agentic AI."
image: images/profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 16em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:sghillman@gmail.com
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=kKydRfAAAAAJ
    - icon: person-badge
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0267-4837
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/samhillman
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/samghillman
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <script type="application/ld+json">
      {
        "@context": "https://schema.org",
        "@type": "ProfilePage",
        "mainEntity": {
          "@type": "Person",
          "name": "Sam Hillman",
          "givenName": "Sam",
          "familyName": "Hillman",
          "jobTitle": "Real-World Evidence Associate Director",
          "description": "Real-world evidence and AI scientist in oncology drug development, specialising in observational study design, causal inference, and agentic AI for automated evidence generation.",
          "url": "https://samhillman.github.io",
          "image": "https://samhillman.github.io/images/profile.jpg",
          "email": "mailto:sghillman@gmail.com",
          "worksFor": {
            "@type": "Organization",
            "name": "AstraZeneca"
          },
          "alumniOf": [
            {
              "@type": "CollegeOrUniversity",
              "name": "University of Edinburgh"
            },
            {
              "@type": "CollegeOrUniversity",
              "name": "Cardiff University"
            }
          ],
          "homeLocation": {
            "@type": "Place",
            "name": "Barcelona, Spain"
          },
          "knowsAbout": [
            "Real-world evidence",
            "Pharmacoepidemiology",
            "Causal inference",
            "Target trial emulation",
            "External control arms",
            "Oncology",
            "Agentic AI",
            "R programming",
            "Electronic health records"
          ],
          "sameAs": [
            "https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0267-4837",
            "https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=kKydRfAAAAAJ",
            "https://github.com/samhillman",
            "https://www.linkedin.com/in/samghillman"
          ]
        }
      }
      </script>
---

I'm a real-world evidence (RWE) and AI scientist working in oncology drug development. I'm an
RWE Associate Director at AstraZeneca in Barcelona. I work on real-world epidemiology across
women's cancers — breast, ovarian, and endometrial — for clinical trial planning, regulatory
agency submission, and health technology assessment (HTA).

Most of my work is about generating regulatory-relevant evidence from electronic health record
and claims data: designing and delivering observational studies and external control arms, and
applying causal inference methods — target trial emulation, propensity-score matching, IPTW,
clone-censor-weight — to contextualise clinical trials and inform investment and regulatory
decisions. I work on the applied use of AI in evidence generation: automating as much as
possible, and designing AI-ready data pipelines, internal tooling, and agentic frameworks.

Before industry, I was a post-doctoral data analyst working on the COVID response in Scotland.
I hold a PhD from the University of Edinburgh in Ecology and Evolutionary Biology, where I
worked on wild wood mice — social network analysis, parasite epidemiology, and statistical
modelling.

I've published in *The Lancet Respiratory Medicine*, *Nature Communications*, *The Lancet
Regional Health – Europe*, and *JCO Precision Oncology*, and I work primarily in R.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
I'm based in Barcelona, Spain. The best way to reach me is by
[email](mailto:sghillman@gmail.com) or [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/samghillman).
:::
